title: Multi-Query Attention (MQA)
description:
Implement Multi-Query Attention (MQA), an extreme variant of GQA where all query heads share a single K head and a single V head.

In standard MHA:
- H query heads, H key heads, H value heads

In MQA (Shazeer, 2019):
- H query heads, but only 1 key head and 1 value head
- KV cache size reduced by factor H
- Each query head independently attends to the same K, V

Weight matrices:
- W_Q: (H * d_k, d_model)
- W_K: (d_k, d_model)  — single K head
- W_V: (d_k, d_model)  — single V head
- W_O: (d_model, H * d_k)

The output is the concatenation of all query heads' attention outputs, then projected by W_O.
constraints: W_K and W_V project to (seq_len, d_k) — single head
Each query head attends to the same K and V
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads, mask=None):
    """Multi-Query Attention (single K, V shared by all query heads).

    Args:
        x: Input (seq_len, d_model)
        W_Q: Query projection (num_heads * d_k, d_model)
        W_K: Key projection (d_k, d_model) — single head
        W_V: Value projection (d_k, d_model) — single head
        W_O: Output projection (d_model, num_heads * d_k)
        num_heads: Number of query heads
        mask: Optional boolean mask (seq_len, seq_len)

    Returns:
        output: (seq_len, d_model)
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads, mask=None):
    seq_len, d_model = x.shape
    d_k = d_model // num_heads

    # Project Q: (seq_len, num_heads, d_k)
    Q = (x @ W_Q.T).reshape(seq_len, num_heads, d_k).transpose(1, 0, 2)  # (H, seq, d_k)

    # Project K, V: single head each (seq_len, d_k)
    K = x @ W_K.T  # (seq_len, d_k)
    V = x @ W_V.T  # (seq_len, d_k)

    # Compute attention scores for each query head against the shared K
    # Q: (H, seq_q, d_k), K: (seq_k, d_k)
    scores = Q @ K.T / np.sqrt(d_k)  # (H, seq_q, seq_k)

    if mask is not None:
        scores = np.where(mask[None], -1e9, scores)

    exp_s = np.exp(scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True))
    w = exp_s / exp_s.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)  # (H, seq_q, seq_k)

    # Attended values: each head uses the same V
    out = w @ V  # (H, seq_q, d_k)

    # Concat and project
    out = out.transpose(1, 0, 2).reshape(seq_len, num_heads * d_k)
    return out @ W_O.T
explanation:
Multi-Query Attention (Shazeer, 2019) was one of the first techniques to reduce KV cache memory for faster inference.

Key insight: different query heads need different projections of Q to learn diverse attention patterns, but they can share the same K and V representation without much quality loss.

Memory reduction: KV cache goes from O(H * seq_len * d_k) in MHA to O(seq_len * d_k) in MQA — a factor of H reduction.

Quality trade-off: MQA slightly underperforms MHA, which led to the development of GQA as a middle ground.

Implementation: W_K and W_V project to d_k (not H*d_k), giving a single K and V. Then each query head attends to this shared K, V. The operation Q @ K.T (with Q: H, seq, d_k and K: seq, d_k) works directly by broadcasting.

test_cases.name: test_output_shape
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
seq, d_model, num_heads = 8, 32, 4
d_k = d_model // num_heads
x = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(num_heads*d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, num_heads*d_k)*0.1
out = multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert out.shape == (seq, d_model)

test_cases.name: test_causal_mask
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(1)
seq, d_model, num_heads = 6, 16, 2
d_k = d_model // num_heads
x = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(num_heads*d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, num_heads*d_k)*0.1
mask = np.triu(np.ones((seq, seq), dtype=bool), k=1)
out = multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads, mask=mask)
assert out.shape == (seq, d_model)
assert np.all(np.isfinite(out))

test_cases.name: test_single_head_case
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(2)
seq, d_model, num_heads = 5, 16, 1
d_k = d_model
x = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, d_k)*0.1
out = multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert out.shape == (seq, d_model)

test_cases.name: test_different_input_different_output
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(3)
seq, d_model, num_heads = 4, 16, 2
d_k = d_model // num_heads
W_Q = np.random.randn(num_heads*d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_O = np.random.randn(d_model, num_heads*d_k)*0.1
x1 = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
x2 = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
out1 = multi_query_attention(x1, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
out2 = multi_query_attention(x2, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert not np.allclose(out1, out2)

test_cases.name: test_zero_wo_zero_output
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
seq, d_model, num_heads = 4, 16, 2
d_k = d_model // num_heads
x = np.random.randn(seq, d_model)
W_Q = np.random.randn(num_heads*d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_K = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_V = np.random.randn(d_k, d_model)*0.1
W_O = np.zeros((d_model, num_heads*d_k))
out = multi_query_attention(x, W_Q, W_K, W_V, W_O, num_heads)
assert np.allclose(out, 0)

hints: W_K and W_V have shape (d_k, d_model) — they project to a single head's dimension
Q has shape (H, seq, d_k), K has shape (seq, d_k)
scores = Q @ K.T — numpy broadcasts so K.T (d_k, seq) works with Q (H, seq, d_k)
